purpose: Direct-stage hardware test of per-stage memory-token hypotheses for the remaining playability failures.

candidates.name: tnd58mem_gfxvtx_bal
candidates.intent: Increase gfx/vtx pools for Party, Hotel, City, Volcano, and The End while lowering texture cache enough to keep total named allocations near the current budget.

results.result: Still briefly reaches a first-person Party frame, then falls to blue rectangle/black. No useful improvement over tnd58.
Still reaches Hotel geometry but alternates with sky/white-blue/prism-style corrupt frames.
Still blue transition rectangle then black, no useful world render.
Still live-renders Volcano but keeps the prism/blown-out frame family.
Still blue transition rectangle then black, no useful world render.

candidates.name: tnd58mem_mtdown
candidates.intent: Lower texture-cache budgets only, testing whether the failures are caused by general stage-bank pressure.

results.result: Worse than tnd58/gfxvtx_bal: blue rectangle/black without the earlier useful live Party frame.
Still corrupts between Hotel geometry and blown-out/sky frames.
Still blue transition rectangle then black.
Still shows the Volcano prism/blown-out corruption family.
Do not trust as an End result: SC64 upload reported a timeout and the capture shows the SC64 menu.

candidates.name: tnd58mem_gfxvtx_keep
candidates.intent: Increase gfx/vtx pools without lowering texture cache, isolating display-list/vertex pressure at the cost of more stage RAM.

results.result: Worse than tnd58/gfxvtx_bal: capture shows SC64 menu before cycle, then blue rectangle/black with no useful Party render.
Still corrupts, with sky/prism edge artifacts and normal Hotel frames alternating.
Still renders Volcano but shows bad gray/white frame-state bars and does not cure the corruption.

conclusion: Per-stage token changes do not fix the major failure groups.
Texture-cache reduction alone regresses Party and does not fix Hotel/Volcano/City.
Gfx/vtx increases, with or without texture-cache balancing, do not fix Party, City, The End, Hotel, or Volcano.
The remaining root cause is more likely a shared render/camera/VI/framebuffer state issue or stage transition ownership issue than simple per-stage -mgfx/-mvtx/-mt/-ma sizing.

restore.sc64_state_after_restore: Direct ROM mode, EEPROM 4k; upload succeeded and sc64deployer info reported CIC compare algorithm.
